review: Same 10 songs keep repeating on my daily mix. I've heard Kesariya probably 500 times this month. Where's the variety?
The recommendation algorithm is terrible. It just plays whatever is trending on Bollywood charts. I want to discover new indie artists but Gaana has no idea what I like.
App is okay but the autoplay feature keeps playing the same artists. Spotify's Discover Weekly is miles ahead. Gaana needs something similar.
I created 5 different playlists to tell the app my taste and it STILL recommends mainstream Bollywood. Very frustrating.
The music library is huge but I can never find anything new. The explore section just shows trending songs, not personalized picks.
Good app overall but wish the radio feature was smarter. It just plays random songs with no connection to what I was listening to.
Worst recommendation system I've ever seen. I listen to classical fusion and it recommends Yo Yo Honey Singh. Makes no sense.
Why does Gaana keep recommending the same 50 popular songs? I want to find hidden gems, underground artists, new releases from small artists.
Discovery is the biggest weakness. I switched from Spotify and I miss Discover Weekly badly. Gaana has no equivalent feature.
The app has no memory. I skip a song and it plays the same song again 10 minutes later. The algorithm doesn't learn from my behavior.
Playlist recommendations are stuck in 2020. They keep recommending old Bollywood. No awareness of new independent music scene.
App works fine but I wish there was a mood-based discovery feature. Like if I'm working, suggest focus music. Currently it just plays whatever.
Downloaded because of the regional music library but the recommendation engine shows no regional content. It's all Hindi mainstream.
The weekly mixes are disappointing. Same songs every week. Not learning what I like at all. My listening history means nothing to the algorithm.
I love Punjabi music but Gaana recommends me Tamil songs I never listen to. The personalization is completely broken.
Been using for 3 months. The app still doesn't know my taste. Every recommendation feels random. No intelligence behind it.
Decent app but the explore feature is useless for discovery. It's just a trending chart. I want AI-powered suggestions based on my mood.
Why is there no 'because you listened to X' feature? Spotify does this perfectly. Gaana just dumps popular songs on me.
The radio feature is a disaster. I start a radio from one song and within 3 tracks it's playing completely unrelated songs.
Good music library but poor discovery tools. I end up going to YouTube to find new songs then come back to Gaana to listen.
I follow 20 artists but Gaana never notifies me about their new releases. Discovery is completely passive, no proactive features.
The app treats all users the same. Whether you listen to ghazals or EDM, you get the same Bollywood recommendations. Zero personalization.
Nice interface but the AI needs a lot of work. The more I use it, the worse the recommendations seem to get. Very counterintuitive.
I specifically disliked certain songs but they still show up in my feed. The feedback loop is completely broken.
Switched back to Spotify after 2 months. Gaana's discovery is 5 years behind. No contextual recommendations, no mood detection, nothing.
The app is fine for playing songs I already know. But for finding new music? Completely useless. Just shows what's popular.
My daily mix is literally the same 20 songs every single day. What's the point of calling it a 'mix'?
Good regional content but the recommendation engine doesn't understand regional preferences. Mixes Telugu and Tamil randomly.
The algorithm has no context awareness. I listen to sad songs after a breakup and it starts my morning with the same sad playlist. No mood detection.
Been requesting a 'new releases from artists I follow' feature for years. Still not there. How does Gaana not have this basic feature?
Discovery Weekly equivalent would transform this app. Right now I rely on friends for music recommendations, not Gaana.
The personalization is a joke. I explicitly rate songs and it still recommends similar songs I've rated badly. No learning happening.
Autoplay after playlist ends is terrible. Completely random songs with no connection to my taste or the playlist I was listening to.
Solid app but discovery features are 2015-era. No AI, no context, no personalization. Just popularity-based recommendations.
I listen to instrumental music mostly. Gaana algorithm has never once recommended instrumental music to me. Completely ignores my habits.
The search is good for songs I know. But there's nothing to help me discover what I DON'T know. That's the gap.
Radio stations are just curated by humans, not AI. The same playlist recycled. No dynamic personalization.
My teenage daughter and I share the account. The algorithm gets confused and recommends nothing useful for either of us.
Great for regional language content availability. But the recommendation system doesn't understand multi-lingual listeners at all.
I've been on Gaana for 4 years. The recommendations in 2026 are the same as 2022. No improvement. Stuck in time.
The 'Similar Artists' feature recommends artists that are not similar at all. No understanding of music genre or style.
Good app, bad AI. The difference between Gaana and Spotify is entirely in the recommendation intelligence. Gaana loses badly.
Every Monday I check 'For You' and every Monday I'm disappointed. Same songs, same artists, nothing new.
I told the app I like jazz fusion. It recommended Hindi pop. The onboarding preference setting means absolutely nothing.
The trending section is fine. But where's the 'based on your taste' section? That's what users actually want.
I like that Gaana has a lot of regional content. But I can never discover new regional artists because the algorithm doesn't surface them.
Playlist shuffle is broken. It plays the first 5 songs of every playlist repeatedly and never reaches the rest.
The 'New Releases' section shows the same songs for weeks. No freshness, no real-time updates to recommendations.
Good music catalog but the app makes it hard to explore. Everything is hidden behind trending charts and paid promotions.
I've tried to give the app feedback by liking and disliking songs for 6 months. Zero impact on recommendations. Feedback goes nowhere.
